benchmarks.comparison: OpenNN vs PyTorch vs TensorFlow on the canonical HIGGS dense classifier shape (28->1024->1024->1, BCE, Adam; synthetic contract-shaped data), train and infer max batch, fp32 and bf16; --device cpu runs the same matrix fp32 under a hard RLIMIT_DATA cap
needs: GPU infer final 2026-07-06 (RTX 3060 Laptop 6GB, WSL2, real HIGGS rows): untiled opennn 695980 fp32 / 1352555 bf16 > pytorch 691200 / 1344512 > tensorflow 451260 / 448237; opennn tiled resident 45203456 fp32 / 48234496 bf16 (tile 65536 auto, tail-arena aliasing, dtype-sized output, single-tile output dedup) at fp32 5.22M/s (fastest measured) / bf16 9.78M/s; rerun on the reference GPU

DONE 2026-07-06: 40-agent adversarial VRAM audit (35 findings, 13 confirmed): implemented tile-65536 auto + tail-arena overlay + bf16-width/single-tile output + lazy legacy cuBLAS handle; formally confirmed the untiled activation floor; deferred with recorded numbers: bf16 input-cast staging aliased into dead h2 slot (+1.33% bf16 untiled, needs a destination-hint through linear_forward_gpu) and freeing the fp32 parameter master after bf16 cast (+0.07%, needs re-materialization guards on 4 training paths)
GPU untiled VRAM slope fully accounted (fp32 8302 B/sample = activations+input; bf16 +51 B/sample fp32->bf16 input staging, unavoidable under the fp32-features protocol)

DONE 2026-07-06: lazy cuDNN handle (device_backend: created on first cuDNN op; dense-only nets save ~26 MiB fixed VRAM, measured 135->109 MiB at batch 1024); untiled ceiling unchanged on WSL2 (driver demotes cold pages near the cap; rerun 694444/1348973, artifact gpu-higgs-max-batch-20260705T230710Z.json) -- expect ~+3k fp32 samples on native Linux; SetZeroAsyncClearsHostBuffer failure verified pre-existing via stash rebuild

run CPU train mode (CPU infer measured 2026-07-05 under WSL2 at 8 GiB RLIMIT_DATA: opennn 62914560 mkl tiled / 1013085 eigen untiled / 1012070 mkl untiled > pytorch 837263 > tensorflow 524288; MKL is the production CPU config)

GPU train measured 2026-07-06: monolithic opennn 348245 fp32 / 674998 bf16 > pytorch 346112 / 650834 > tensorflow 290757 / 261618; opennn gradient-accumulated step >= 67108864 both precisions (search limit; VRAM constant 1183/695 MiB) and FASTER than monolithic (fp32 1.19M vs 1.03M samples/s, bf16 1.97M vs 1.75M)

DONE 2026-07-06: AdaptiveMomentEstimation::set_update_period(K) gradient accumulation: K equal mini-batch gradients averaged into a parameters-sized accumulator, one scaled Adam update = exact virtual-batch step (verified single-thread CPU: 0.69913 monolithic vs 0.699131 accumulated); guarded against the CUDA-graph path; warmup resets the accumulator; monolithic-vs-accumulated GPU loss prints differ only via the pre-existing warmup update asymmetry
deferred: bf16 training Batch::fp32_staging retains ~224 B/sample on device (two fp32 input copies feeding the bf16 cast) -- ~+2.7% bf16 monolithic train if freed after cast

CPU train monolithic measured 2026-07-06 (8 GiB RLIMIT_DATA, fp32): opennn 501651 > pytorch 411423 > tensorflow 331784 (+22%/+51%; widest like-for-like margin -- shared 16.5 KB/sample slope, opennn ~20 MiB baseline vs pytorch ~1.5 GiB); accumulated search in progress

tile-throughput analysis (workflow, 2026-07-06): defaults proven optimal -- GPU fp32 65536 is a cuBLASLt per-shape effect (plan cache takes the top-1 heuristic; the 32768 cliff is an algorithm bucket), bf16 and CPU tiles cannot beat untiled (fixed ~67us/tile vs L2-resident bf16 weights; monotone MKL efficiency); FOLLOW-UP: time ~8 cuBLASLt heuristic candidates during the untimed warmup instead of requested=1 (device_backend.cpp get_lt_matmul_plan) -- likely lifts fp32 throughput across all configurations and makes it tile-robust

DONE 2026-07-05: row-tiled CPU inference (neural_network.cpp calculate_outputs: batch-separable forward in tiles under a 1 GiB activation budget, never more memory than untiled; trial reuses tile workspaces resident-style, 131072-row tiles = measured MKL speed-parity point, medians 168k tiled vs 171.6k untiled samples/s); max batch 1012070 -> 62914560 (boundary at 1M resolution, 63963136 fails); suites unchanged (593/595 eigen, 592/595 mkl)

MKL build note: CorrelationsTest.LogisticCorrelation fails under MKL (logistic fit converges sign-flipped, coefficient -1 vs +1) -- MKL-numerics sensitivity in the correlations utility, independent of the dense forward path; investigate separately
rerun CPU with a torch CPU-only wheel (venv wheel is cu121; its baseline understates PyTorch, though even a zero baseline caps it at ~1.03M)

DONE 2026-07-05: fixed CPU linear_forward Eigen temporary (tensor_operations.cpp: GEMM into the output slot + in-place rowwise bias add); opennn CPU infer max batch 615712 -> 1013085, throughput 24k -> 154k samples/s at batch 262144; tests 593/595 (2 failures pre-existing, Scaling/Unscaling, unrelated); guarded by tests/linear_forward_memory_test.cpp (RLIMIT_DATA-capped steady-state forward, verified to fail on the reverted bug)
replaces the historical Rosenbrock dense max-batch as the dense capacity probe (DENSE_HIGGS_MIGRATION.md)

lifecycle_values.headline_candidate: Can appear in the public headline index after raw commands/artifacts are archived.
lifecycle_values.supporting_only: Useful context, but not a primary performance headline.
lifecycle_values.internal_only: Useful for migration, development, or presentation drafts; not a public claim.
lifecycle_values.hold_back: Do not use externally until the listed rerun or methodology fix is complete.
lifecycle_values.historical: Retained as engineering history; superseded for new claims.
